event: positive rapid test for Group A Streptococcus
respiratory rate of 24 breaths per minute
oxygen saturation of 100% on room air
mottling and cyanosis of cheeks, chest, and neck
no change in blood pressure, heart rate, or dusky mottling
diastolic collapse of the right atrium
diastolic collapse of the right ventricle
precise evaluation of the inferior vena cava not possible
decision made to proceed with bedside pericardiocentesis
drained 200 milliliters of bloody pericardial fluid
open pericardiotomy and pericardial window placement
slightly increased voltage of QRS complexes
analysis of pericardial biopsy and pericardial effusion fluid
revealed adenocarcinoma with immune stain suggesting a pulmonary primary source
bronchial brushing and washing suggested of adenocarcinoma
final results of blood, urine, and bronchial-alveolar washing cultures negative
discharged to follow up as an outpatient at the oncology clinic
